name: Checkr API
what_it_does: Background check and candidate screening platform for employment, gig economy, and tenant screening. API supports creating candidates, ordering background check packages (criminal, MVR, employment verification, drug screening), monitoring report status, and receiving webhook notifications when reports complete.
use_cases: Automate pre-employment background screening in ATS or HRIS workflows
Trigger background checks for gig workers onboarding to marketplace platforms
Monitor background check report status and route results to hiring managers
Retrieve adverse action workflow steps for candidates who don't pass screening
Integrate continuous monitoring alerts for existing worker populations
not_for: Consumer self-directed background checks (Checkr is B2B only; requires business account)
Instant identity verification in real-time user flows (reports take hours to days)
International background checks in countries not supported by Checkr's network
best_when: Building HR automation or gig platform onboarding that needs to programmatically order, track, and act on background check results with compliance safeguards built in.
avoid_when: You need instant results (criminal checks can take 1-5 business days), you're screening individuals in unsupported countries, or you're a small business that doesn't meet Checkr's permissible purpose requirements.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic Auth using API key as username with empty password. Separate keys for sandbox and production environments. OAuth available for Checkr Partner integrations (marketplace platforms connecting customer accounts).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox environment is free and fully functional for testing. No free production checks.
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-check pricing based on package type. Criminal checks start around $25-35. MVR and employment verifications are add-ons. Volume discounts available.
pricing.notes: Production access requires business verification and permissible purpose attestation. Pricing is usage-based with no subscription fee, but a sales process is typically required for production access.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented. In practice, background check creation is the bottleneck (throughput limited by fulfillment, not API rate limits).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses are clear and include field-level validation messages. FCRA-related errors have specific codes that indicate compliance steps required.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Report creation is not idempotent — submitting the same candidate twice creates two reports. Agents must track candidate IDs and report IDs to avoid duplicate charges.
known_agent_gotchas: FCRA compliance requirements mean agents cannot auto-reject candidates based on report results — adverse action workflow is legally mandated and must be human-in-the-loop
Background check reports are not instant — agents must use webhooks or polling; report turnaround ranges from minutes to days
Sandbox and production environments have different API keys and different base URLs; easy to accidentally point at wrong environment
Candidate PII (SSN, DOB) must be submitted via a hosted iframe or secure tokenization — cannot POST raw SSN via server-side API in most integration patterns
Report statuses have multiple intermediate states (pending, consider, clear, suspended, dispute); agent state machines must handle all transitions

security_score_components.security_notes: Handles extremely sensitive PII (SSN, criminal history). SOC 2 Type II certified. FCRA compliance is enforced at the product level. PII collection via hosted secure forms reduces server-side exposure. Single API key has no granular scope control.
